comments.content:
Proposed precursor for 9-(methylsulfinyl)nonyl glucosinolate / glucoarabin (68)
Assuming the existence of a heptahomomethionine-derived pathway, we propose that 9-methylthiononyl glucosinolate (89) represents a potential precursor of 9-(methylsulfinyl)nonyl glucosinolate/glucoarabin (68) .  Based on analysis in MZmine v4.5.1 and SIRIUS v6.1.1, analysis performed on data from Felicia C. Wolters (manuscript in preparation) by Manze Yu.

Additional chain-elongated precursors
We propose heptahomomethionine and octahomomethionine as potential candidate precursor amino acids for aliphatic GSL biosynthesis.  Based on analysis in MZmine v4.5.1 and SIRIUS v6.1.1, analysis performed on data from Felicia C. Wolters (manuscript in preparation) by Manze Yu.

By analogy with the conversion steps observed in pentahomomethionine- and hexahomomethionine-derived pathways, we propose that the potential immediate precursor of 6-methylthiohexyl glucosinolate/ glucolesquerellin (88) is 6-methylthiohexyl desulfoglucosinolate/ desulfo-glucolesquerellin (d88). Based on analysis in MZmine v4.5.1 and SIRIUS v6.1.1, analysis performed on data from Felicia C. Wolters (manuscript in preparation) by Manze Yu.

Additional chain-elongated precursors
We propose heptahomomethionine and octahomomethionine as potential candidate precursor amino acids for aliphatic GSL biosynthesis.  Based on analysis in MZmine v4.5.1 and SIRIUS v6.1.1, analysis performed on data from Felicia C. Wolters (manuscript in preparation) by Manze Yu.

Proposed end product of the octahomomethionine-derived pathway
Assuming the existence of an octahomomethionine-derived pathway, we propose 10-(methylsulfinyl)decyl glucosinolate/ glucocamelinin (65) as a potential end product .  Based on analysis in MZmine v4.5.1 and SIRIUS v6.1.1, analysis performed on data from Felicia C. Wolters (manuscript in preparation) by Manze Yu.

publicationXrefa1e.standardName: Desulfoglucosinolate sulfotransferases from Arabidopsis thaliana catalyze the final step in the biosynthesis of the glucosinolate core structure.

publicationXrefbe2.standardName: Kinetics and substrate specificities of desulfo-glucosinolate sulfotransferases in Arabidopsis thaliana.

publicationXrefc11.standardName: Arabidopsis glucosyltransferase UGT74B1 functions in glucosinolate biosynthesis and auxin homeostasis.

publicationXrefc85.standardName: The three desulfoglucosinolate sulfotransferase proteins in Arabidopsis have different substrate specificities and are differentially expressed.

publicationXrefcb1.standardName: Biosynthesis of methionine-derived glucosinolates in Arabidopsis thaliana: recombinant expression and characterization of methylthioalkylmalate synthase, the condensing enzyme of the chain-elongation cycle.

publicationXrefe11.standardName: MAM3 catalyzes the formation of all aliphatic glucosinolate chain lengths in Arabidopsis.

publicationXrefe12.standardName: Comparison of glucosinolate diversity in the crucifer tribe Cardamineae and the remaining order Brassicales highlights repetitive evolutionary loss and gain of biosynthetic steps.

publicationXrefe25.standardName: Glucosinolate breakdown in Arabidopsis: mechanism, regulation and biological significance.

publicationXreff7e.standardName: Glucosinolate structural diversity, identification, chemical synthesis and metabolism in plants.

publicationXreffb2.standardName: Arabidopsis thaliana encodes a bacterial-type heterodimeric isopropylmalate isomerase involved in both Leu biosynthesis and the Met chain elongation pathway of glucosinolate formation.

pathway.author: [Mikikot, Afukushima, Khanspers, Mkutmon, Eweitz, ManzeYu, FeliciaWolters]

comments.content: This pathway describes the glucosinolate derived from methionine in Arabidopsis thaliana. This is based on the KEGG pathway and PlantCyc. Some pathway entities are unknown and being investigated, these are denoted as "Catalyst" and "Metabolite" for now.

pathway.name: Glucosinolate biosynthesis from methionine
